Node.help: The base class that defines the common characteristics of all aptixia network stack element plugins.

PropertyNodeList.name: childrenList
PropertyNodeList.help: The list of StackElementPlugins that are 'stacked' on top of this StackElementPlugin.

PropertyString.name: internalStackElementId
PropertyString.help: This property is for internal use only. It maintains a Guid associated with the stack element for use in port cpu layer creation and destruction
A textual 'legend' that can be used to label the plugin.
PropertyString.name: comment

Struct.name: CardPluginInfo
Struct.help: Information relating to the ixia card associated with this plugin (if any).

Member.name: name
Member.help: The specific name of the card

Member.name: type
Member.help: The general type of the card

Struct.name: SupportedCardInfo
Struct.help: Information about cards that can be used with this plugin.

Member.name: type
Member.help: The general type of the card.

Member.name: maxInterfaces
Member.help: The maximum number of interfaces that can be created on this card.

Struct.name: PluginInfo
Struct.help: Contains high level information relating to plugin such as name, version, card compatibility, etc.

Member.name: sourceLocation
Member.help: The location (on disk) of this plugin.

Member.name: name
Member.help: Legend/Caption style text describing the plugin

Member.name: pluginClass
Member.help: The general class of the plugin

Member.name: compatibleCards
Member.help: List of cards that are compatible with this plugin (if any).

Member.name: canStackOnBaseStyles
Member.help:
'canStackOnBaseStyles' is a list of base-stacking-styles this plugin is able stack on top of.
When this plugin tries to stack on top of another plugin, it cross checks it own 'canStackOnBaseStyles' against other plugin's 'canStackOnBaseStyles'.
If the cross check finds an intersection between the other plugin's 'canStackOnBaseStyles' and this plugin's 'canUseBaseStyles', then this plugin is allowed to stack on top of other plugin
Note: "Root" is a special value for 'canUseBaseStyles' which means that this plugin can be used as the root of a stacking tree (it needs no base below it)

Member.name: canProvideBaseStyles
Member.help:
'canProvideBaseStyles' is a list of base-stacking-styles this plugin can support.
When another plugin tries to stack onto this plugin, it cross checks the other plugin's 'canUseBaseStyles' list against it's own 'canProvideBaseStyles' list
If the cross check finds an intersection between the other plugin's 'canUseBaseStyles' and this plugin's 'canProvideBaseStyles', then the other plugin is allowed to stack on top of this plugin
Note: This plugin's 'Type' value is provided as a built-in base-stacking-style In addition to the styles defined in 'canProvideBaseStyle' member

Member.name: supportedCards
Member.help: Cards allowed by this plugin. An empty list means the plugin supports all cards. strings in the list are the form as seen in explorer, e.g. '10/100/1000 ALM T8'

Method2.name: Echo
Method2.help: This diagnostic method is for internal use only

Argument.name: value
Argument.help: This diagnostic method param is for internal use only

Argument.name: rval
Argument.help: This diagnostic method param is for internal use only

Method2.name: DirectoryInfo
Method2.help: Retrieve general "directory" style information for this plugin

Argument.name: rval
Argument.help: The requested plugin information. See 'StackElementPlugin.PluginInfo' structure definition for more info.

Method2.name: InsertPluginParent
Method2.help: Insert a new plugin into the stack as the parent of this plugin

Argument.name: pluginType
Argument.help: The type of new plugin to create (as the parent of this plugin)

Argument.name: newPlugin
Argument.help: Output param containing the newly created parent plugin

Method2.name: InsertBeforeCandidates
Method2.help: Return a list of PluginInfo's that describe which plugin's are insertable before this plugin.

Argument.name: candidates
Argument.help: The list of PluginInfo's that describe plugins that are insertable before this plugin.

Method2.name: RemovePluginChild
Method2.help: Remove the n-th child plugin of 'this' plugin. Descendants of the removed plugin are re-attached at the location of the plugin being removed.

Argument.name: index
Argument.help: The index of the plugin to remove from the stack
